front: Work: slideshow-turned-photobook documenting a 'recreated family' of drag queens and bohemians in 1980s New York; title comes from a song in Brecht and Weill's Threepenny Opera; dedicated to the photographer's sister, who committed suicide
back: The Ballad of Sexual Dependency (Nan Goldin)

front: Artist: founded P.A.I.N. to protest the Sackler family's role in the opioid crisis; staged die-ins at museums including the Guggenheim and the Louvre
Artist: diaristic photographer of New York queer and no-wave subcultures; photographed drag queens including Misty and Jimmy Paulette in a Taxi, NYC
Work: 2022 Laura Poitras documentary about this photographer's activism against the Sackler family
back: All the Beauty and the Bloodshed (about Nan Goldin)

front: Artist: depicted her own bruised face in a photograph taken one month after being battered by a boyfriend
Artist: first solo exhibition depicted the Boston transgender community (e.g., Ivy wearing a fall, Boston)
Artist: curated the 1989 exhibition Witnesses: Against Our Vanishing, one of the first major artistic responses to the AIDS crisis
Work: originally a slideshow partly set to Velvet Underground music; the cover image (Goldin in bed with a smoking shirtless man) appeared on the title page of Greer Lankton's installation
back: The Ballad of Sexual Dependency (Nan Goldin)
